subject: SC Implementation - Simplot - Import URLs as Images/Docs

turns.author: Jaime Lawrence <jaime.lawrence@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text: Please import all URL links as images/docs for the items.       This request can be escalated to Justin as it was already discussed.
turns.body_html:
Please import all URL links as images/docs for the items.  

This request can be escalated to Justin as it was already discussed.

turns.author: "SchoolCafé " <customercare@schoolcafe.com>
turns.body_text: Notes for this:      We will match their SKU against our Product Code field, which should be unique for all existing items.  We will programmatically convert their WEBP images into JPG files before importing.  If possible, we will modify the file names of the documents to include suffixes to differentiate the two document types.  Customer has requested the following  #####_Standard Spec Sheet.pdf for the Spec Sheet files  ######_K12 Spec Sheet.jpg for the Product Formulation Sheet files      We will tag the images and documents with our "Use As" flags from the Images & Docs section  Images will be tagged with 'Preferred Ingredient Image' and 'Preferred Menu Image'  Spec Sheets will be tagged with 'Spec Sheet' (we're adding this option to the dropdown)  K12 sheets will be tagged with 'Product Formulation Sheet'      Please let me know if we need to cover anything else. Thank you.
turns.body_html:
Notes for this:

  • We will match their SKU against our Product Code field, which should be unique for all existing items.
  • We will programmatically convert their WEBP images into JPG files before importing.
  • If possible, we will modify the file names of the documents to include suffixes to differentiate the two document types.
    • Customer has requested the following
      • #####_Standard Spec Sheet.pdf for the Spec Sheet files
      • ######_K12 Spec Sheet.jpg for the Product Formulation Sheet files
  • We will tag the images and documents with our "Use As" flags from the Images & Docs section
    • Images will be tagged with 'Preferred Ingredient Image' and 'Preferred Menu Image'
    • Spec Sheets will be tagged with 'Spec Sheet' (we're adding this option to the dropdown)
    • K12 sheets will be tagged with 'Product Formulation Sheet' 
Please let me know if we need to cover anything else. Thank you.

subject: SC Implementation - Southwest ISD - Update all base units to decimals

turns.author: Jaime Lawrence <jaime.lawrence@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text: Southwest ISD would like to update all base units for their items to be counted by decimals.  When I use the bulk tool I am unable to do so.       Currently the district has started their physical inventories.  I first tested just the milk items, added them to a session, updated to decimal, completed the updates, exited the session. All milk items showed correctly in the physical inventories.       Next I added all 755 items to a bulk update session.  I selected decimal for all items on all pages.  When I went to save the information I noticed the save weights button is greyed out.  None of the information I entered saved.               I would also like to report quite a bit of slowness.  It took about 10 seconds per item (video attached).  Once I closed all other browsers it did speed up a bit, but still took quite some time to go through all items.       The district has paused all of their inventory counts until these can be adjusted.
turns.body_html:
Southwest ISD would like to update all base units for their items to be counted by decimals.  When I use the bulk tool I am unable to do so.  

Currently the district has started their physical inventories.  I first tested just the milk items, added them to a session, updated to decimal, completed the updates, exited the session. All milk items showed correctly in the physical inventories.  

Next I added all 755 items to a bulk update session.  I selected decimal for all items on all pages.  When I went to save the information I noticed the save weights button is greyed out.  None of the information I entered saved.  



I would also like to report quite a bit of slowness.  It took about 10 seconds per item (video attached).  Once I closed all other browsers it did speed up a bit, but still took quite some time to go through all items.  

The district has paused all of their inventory counts until these can be adjusted.

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text: Southwest ISD would like to update all base units for their items to be counted by decimals.  When trying to use the bulk tool, we are unable to do so; the button is grayed out.      escalating per Alekya
turns.body_html:
Southwest ISD would like to update all base units for their items to be counted by decimals.  When trying to use the bulk tool, we are unable to do so; the button is grayed out.

escalating per Alekya

turns.author: "SchoolCafé " <customercare@schoolcafe.com>
turns.body_text: Hi Jaime Lawrence      We have updated all inventory base units to decimals and closed the session.
turns.body_html:
Hi Jaime Lawrence 

We have updated all inventory base units to decimals and closed the session.

turns.body_html:
Escalating for review. Ingredient LI-0089 (Spaghetti Pasta) will not show up in Item Management ingredient searches in the main table or in other recipes.
  • LI-0089 can be seen attached to recipe LR-0255 (Spaghetti and Meatballs with Breadstick) to access
  • LI-0089 does not pull up in searches within a recipe nor under Item Management (Items, recipes, Menu items) by code or partia
  • LI-0089 initially did not have serving size or nutritional information. Leigh Ann added - no change
    • LI-0089 Ingredient report shows nutritionals blank still.
  • District added additional info to other categories not needed (brand, storage category, contribution, etc - no change
  • Other example items that are searchable yet have little/no details (ie no inventory pack size, cost, storage categories, contributions, , menu info, etc):
    • LI-1078 (Orange Extract)
    • LI-1080 (Chicken and Cheese Cripsy Tacos)
    • LI-0618 (Birria Pupusa) 

References

turns.author: "SchoolCafé " <customercare@schoolcafe.com>
turns.body_text: Hi Michael McComis,     We identified the issue. The item was not searchable because the item type was set to POSButton, which occurred during the POSButton changes. We have now created a new item and attached it to the recipe.  New item code: LI-1087
turns.body_html:
Hi Michael McComis,

We identified the issue. The item was not searchable because the item type was set to POSButton, which occurred during the POSButton changes. We have now created a new item and attached it to the recipe.
New item code: LI-1087

turns.author: agent:22034350878
turns.body_text: Escalating for further review.  Issue: Production record appears to be doing odd things - resaving plan caused a second line for same item on the record, saving counts in the line does not save in record complete (produced count disappears on ketchup), then saving data in line 2 erases line 1.  Reported at 2 sites currently - appears tied to Ketchup item.    Video of glitch:  319511 Southwest ISD Production Record ketchup issue.mp4   Example Site: 042 - Date 5/7 - Lunch - Middle School      Item: Ketchup MI-0314 - appears to have 2 duplicate serving sizes      do not have a screenshot of record before the plan was resaved during troubleshooting - but it was 1 line stating 500 planned, and if values added to the line it cleared and put a red box around produced.  Steps:  Opened Site 042 Sharon Christa McAuliffe Middle School" production calendar and selected 5/7 lunch - middle school plan.  Ashley tried 500 produced, 250 return to stock, 0 waste and save - did not move to record complete and shows a red box around Produced still with no values entered.  Plan count was showing 0 across all columns. Ashley saved 500 to plan count. 2nd line on Production Record appeared.  When saving on 2nd line in production, it applies value to first line. If you save, the value shows on the first line but disappears on 2nd. If you type in the 2nd line it overwrites anything in first line.  1st line now has a "qty prepared" details and 2nd line does not.  Item Management shows 2 lines for same size 1 with cost and 1 without.
turns.body_html:
Escalating for further review.
Issue: Production record appears to be doing odd things - resaving plan caused a second line for same item on the record, saving counts in the line does not save in record complete (produced count disappears on ketchup), then saving data in line 2 erases line 1.  Reported at 2 sites currently - appears tied to Ketchup item. 
  • Video of glitch:  319511 Southwest ISD Production Record ketchup issue.mp4 
  • Example Site: 042 - Date 5/7 - Lunch - Middle School
  • Item: Ketchup MI-0314 - appears to have 2 duplicate serving sizes
  • do not have a screenshot of record before the plan was resaved during troubleshooting - but it was 1 line stating 500 planned, and if values added to the line it cleared and put a red box around produced.
  • Steps:
    • Opened Site 042 Sharon Christa McAuliffe Middle School" production calendar and selected 5/7 lunch - middle school plan.
    • Ashley tried 500 produced, 250 return to stock, 0 waste and save - did not move to record complete and shows a red box around Produced still with no values entered.
    • Plan count was showing 0 across all columns. Ashley saved 500 to plan count. 2nd line on Production Record appeared.
    • When saving on 2nd line in production, it applies value to first line. If you save, the value shows on the first line but disappears on 2nd. If you type in the 2nd line it overwrites anything in first line.
    • 1st line now has a "qty prepared" details and 2nd line does not.
    • Item Management shows 2 lines for same size 1 with cost and 1 without.

subject: SC Implementation - Mankato Area School District ISD 77 - Production Print Recipes scaling serving size

turns.author: Sarah Brook <sbrook1@isd77.org>
turns.body_text: Production>Reports>Production Print Recipe     When using the Scale Recipe by Batch option, the report includes the serving size in the scaling. If the serving size for 1 batch is 1 cup, when scaling the recipe up to 2 batches, it is doubling the serving size to 2 cups as well. See the examples below.     1 batch of LR1425 (serving size 1 cup)       2 batches of LR1425 (shows 2 cup serving size but should remain as 1)  In this example, by doubling the serving size, the rest of the recipe is actually quadrupled instead of doubled.       I tested the Custom Scale Recipe by Batch option and it is pulling correctly. Compare the data in the Custom Scaled Recipe below to the basic Scale Recipe Report above. Note the appropriately double amounts in the Custom as opposed to the quadrupled amounts in basic due to the incorrectly doubled serving size.       I tested LR1429 and it is behaving the same way.      1 batch (Serving Size 1 Each) Scale Recipe by Batch        LR1429 2 Batch (Serving Size is incorrectly populating as 2 Each instead of 1 Each) Scale Recipe by Batch         I tested this in Caring ISD and received the same results (multiplying the Serving Size by the number of batches)
turns.body_html:
Production>Reports>Production Print Recipe

When using the Scale Recipe by Batch option, the report includes the serving size in the scaling. If the serving size for 1 batch is 1 cup, when scaling the recipe up to 2 batches, it is doubling the serving size to 2 cups as well. See the examples below.

1 batch of LR1425 (serving size 1 cup)

2 batches of LR1425 (shows 2 cup serving size but should remain as 1)
In this example, by doubling the serving size, the rest of the recipe is actually quadrupled instead of doubled.

I tested the Custom Scale Recipe by Batch option and it is pulling correctly. Compare the data in the Custom Scaled Recipe below to the basic Scale Recipe Report above. Note the appropriately double amounts in the Custom as opposed to the quadrupled amounts in basic due to the incorrectly doubled serving size.

I tested LR1429 and it is behaving the same way. 

1 batch (Serving Size 1 Each) Scale Recipe by Batch

LR1429 2 Batch (Serving Size is incorrectly populating as 2 Each instead of 1 Each) Scale Recipe by Batch

I tested this in Caring ISD and received the same results (multiplying the Serving Size by the number of batches)

subject: SchoolCafe - Old Colony Regional Vocational Technical - Inventory report to export into a PDF

turns.author: Crystal Andrade <candrade@oldcolony.us>
turns.body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.

After we complete our physical inventory monthly. I would like to print a report to save to refer back to without signing into School Cafe.
These are my steps
Inventory
Reports
Inventory Valuation
Select month- March
Change report type to Valuation Category
Change display to Detailed
Click Generate report.
Export to a PDF
You can scroll through 3 sections.  The last section online has everything, but when you export it to a PDF it only gives you one page of the third section. So when we download it we can not see most of our inventory.

In the end I want a report that breaks down our inventory by food, nonfood & non program food and gives totals for all.


______________________________________________
Crystal Andrade
School Nutrition Director

Old Colony Regional Vocational Technical High School
476 North Avenue
Rochester, MA 02770

(508) 763-8011 ext. 114
candrade@oldcolony.u<mailto:candrade@oldcolony.us>s


When writing or responding, please remember that the Secretary of State's Office has determined that email is public record.

turns.author: "SchoolCafé " <customercare@schoolcafe.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Sarah,     We have identified the reason for the issue. The system is trying to calculate all the recipe information based on weights but there are a few items we have found in the system that do not have any weights and that is throwing the errors. Here are the list of items that need to be corrected for this error to be resolved. Please update these and let us know if you still face an issue. Thanks!   Seasoning Italian 6oz Spice Chili Powder Light Spice Chili Powder Dark Spice Paprika Spanish Fancy Spice Parsley Flakes Spice Thyme Ground            Check out PrimeroEdge Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here  SchoolCafe Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here    Let us know if you have any questions regarding this ticket by
replying all to this ticket
turns.body_html:
Hello Sarah,

We have identified the reason for the issue. The system is trying to calculate all the recipe information based on weights but there are a few items we have found in the system that do not have any weights and that is throwing the errors. Here are the list of items that need to be corrected for this error to be resolved. Please update these and let us know if you still face an issue. Thanks!

subject: Southwest ISD - Items with newly added and unusual serving sizes

turns.author: Ashley Choice <achoice11244@swisd.net>
turns.body_text: District reported during monthly call Menu Items where additional serving sizes without contributions have been appearing.
turns.body_html: District reported during monthly call Menu Items where additional serving sizes without contributions have been appearing.

turns.author: agent:22034350878
turns.body_text: Role permissions were updated so that managers no longer have edit permissions
turns.body_html: Role permissions were updated so that managers no longer have edit permissions

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Ashley,     This is the ticket regarding Items which are showing serving sizes without contributions added in recipes in Item Management. In review during our meeting yesterday it was noted Cafeteria Managers were updating the serving sizes in the items so the role permission was reviewed and Editing was turned off next to the View Item, Recipes, Menu Item options.  You will want to update the menu's to the correct serving size and reassign going forward.   However,in order to fully remove these incorrect serving sizes the Production record would have to be taken out of completion first (such as the 2/25 menu).     As Yolissa suggested, we are escalating a request to our team for review to see if there may be a way for them to review from the back end for converting both the production records and items and if there is perhaps a report to have better clarity on how many production records and items this may apply to than the Post Production costing report we ran.     Let us know if you have any questions regarding this ticket by replying all to this ticket      Check out PrimeroEdge Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here  SchoolCafe Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here        Thank you,  CarrieLee Sparks | CYBERSOFT  Customer Care Specialist, Customer and Expert Care  Support: 866.442.6030 | Email: customercare@primeroedge.com   PrimeroEdge | SchoolCafé  Check out our refresher
training courses in the Primeroedge Academy
turns.body_html:
Hello Ashley,

This is the ticket regarding Items which are showing serving sizes without contributions added in recipes in Item Management. In review during our meeting yesterday it was noted Cafeteria Managers were updating the serving sizes in the items so the role permission was reviewed and Editing was turned off next to the View Item, Recipes, Menu Item options.  You will want to update the menu's to the correct serving size and reassign going forward.   However,in order to fully remove these incorrect serving sizes the Production record would have to be taken out of completion first (such as the 2/25 menu).

As Yolissa suggested, we are escalating a request to our team for review to see if there may be a way for them to review from the back end for converting both the production records and items and if there is perhaps a report to have better clarity on how many production records and items this may apply to than the Post Production costing report we ran.

turns.body_text: Yolissa, sending first to you in case there is anything else you want to add here or I missed anything. Example Item: MI-0705     Additional serving size of 1, 158, 405. On Thu, Mar 19 at 6:33 PM , customercare@primeroedge.com <customercare@primeroedge.com> wrote:
Hello Ashley,

This is the ticket regarding Items which are showing serving sizes without contributions added in recipes in Item Management. In review during our meeting yesterday it was noted Cafeteria Managers were updating the serving sizes in the items so the role permission was reviewed and Editing was turned off next to the View Item, Recipes, Menu Item options.  You will want to update the menu's to the correct serving size and reassign going forward.   However,in order to fully remove these incorrect serving sizes the Production record would have to be taken out of completion first (such as the 2/25 menu).

As Yolissa suggested, we are escalating a request to our team for review to see if there may be a way for them to review from the back end for converting both the production records and items and if there is perhaps a report to have better clarity on how many production records and items this may apply to than the Post Production costing report we ran.

Let us know if you have any questions regarding this ticket by replying all to this ticket

turns.author: agent:22003889558
turns.body_text: Yolissa,  This was deployed and we applied a fix to handle the major cost differential. There are still minor discrepancies that we are seeing with the cost, due to the fact that they used a Standard Measure (Gram, Ounce, Pound) for their serving size description. As you are aware, added standard measures already have a fixed weight that is configured in the system EX: 1 Ounce is 28.35 grams etc. Recipe serving size weights are derived based on the combination of all the weights of the ingredients added into the recipe. Ultimately what is happening is the serving size weight is showing correctly on the recipe (based on ingredients added) but when this recipe gets added into another recipe and we use the serving size description they provided which is ounce, its using the standard calculated gram weight instead of the weight based on ingredients. To resolve these minor discrepancies, we will be appending any serving size descriptions with standard measures to include an "s" so that the system no longer treats them as standard measures and this should fix even the minor discrepancies we are seeing now.      This is the calculated weight based on ingredients:      This is the weight based on using the standard measure of the recipe in a recipe:       Let me know if you need any further clarification on this. Thanks!
turns.body_html:
Yolissa,
This was deployed and we applied a fix to handle the major cost differential. There are still minor discrepancies that we are seeing with the cost, due to the fact that they used a Standard Measure (Gram, Ounce, Pound) for their serving size description. As you are aware, added standard measures already have a fixed weight that is configured in the system EX: 1 Ounce is 28.35 grams etc. Recipe serving size weights are derived based on the combination of all the weights of the ingredients added into the recipe. Ultimately what is happening is the serving size weight is showing correctly on the recipe (based on ingredients added) but when this recipe gets added into another recipe and we use the serving size description they provided which is ounce, its using the standard calculated gram weight instead of the weight based on ingredients. To resolve these minor discrepancies, we will be appending any serving size descriptions with standard measures to include an "s" so that the system no longer treats them as standard measures and this should fix even the minor discrepancies we are seeing now. 

This is the calculated weight based on ingredients: 

This is the weight based on using the standard measure of the recipe in a recipe:

Let me know if you need any further clarification on this. Thanks!

turns.author: "SchoolCafé " <customercare@schoolcafe.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Crystal,     I wanted to follow up regarding the cost discrepancy you reported with the taco meat recipe (#0382). Our development team deployed a fix that addressed the primary issue where the cost was calculating significantly lower when the recipe was used within other recipes.     There may still be very small differences due to the way standard measurements such as ounces or grams are interpreted by the system when a recipe is used as an ingredient. A secondary adjustment has also been applied to help prevent those standard measures from affecting the calculated weight.     When you have a moment, could you please review the affected recipes again (0731, 0730, 0732, 0753, and 0398) and let me know if the cost is now calculating as expected?     If you are still seeing discrepancies, feel free to send an updated screenshot and I will be happy to review further.        Thank you!     Yolissa

turns.body_html:
Hello Crystal,

I wanted to follow up regarding the cost discrepancy you reported with the taco meat recipe (#0382). Our development team deployed a fix that addressed the primary issue where the cost was calculating significantly lower when the recipe was used within other recipes.

There may still be very small differences due to the way standard measurements such as ounces or grams are interpreted by the system when a recipe is used as an ingredient. A secondary adjustment has also been applied to help prevent those standard measures from affecting the calculated weight.

When you have a moment, could you please review the affected recipes again (0731, 0730, 0732, 0753, and 0398) and let me know if the cost is now calculating as expected?

If you are still seeing discrepancies, feel free to send an updated screenshot and I will be happy to review further.
 
Thank you!

turns.body_html:
The district is using planned entrees and noticed an incorrect egg item on breakfast menu (03-02-2026). They understand that menus with completed Production cannot be changed but want the menus updated going forward.

As per the note above from Alyssa Forrester,
The district used Find and Replace to substitute MI-0021 with MI-0494. However, there is no history on the Find and Replace page to show this replacement. Now, only menus where the item cannot be replaced are displayed.

The next occurrence of MI-0021 on the menu is on 3/9. Since they used Find and Replace, MI-0494 should appear on the breakfast 25-26 W2D1 menu. Production plans for 3/9 have not been completed yet.

Although they have replaced the menu item for the allowed menus, the updates are not reflected in the upcoming menus. 


Breakfast 25-26 W2D1 menu (Production - Calendar - site ((1) Colorado School for the Deaf and the Blind) - 03/09/2026 - Breakfast - Main Line



Production plans have not been completed for 3/9


The district has already replaced the menu item for the menus that were approved; however, the updates are not appearing in the upcoming menus.

I have verified that the find and replace function is working as expected in the Caring ISD, and it appears to be functioning correctly. 
I did not conduct any troubleshooting on the Colorado School for the Deaf and Blind site, as it contains real-time information.

But the OVS flags for their production records did not get migrated to SC.

turns.body_text: checking
turns.body_html: checking

turns.author: agent:22003889558
turns.body_text: All,   We realized the the OVS flag in PE is on the menu line and meal type per site. In SC we associate the OVS on the menu itself therefore there is not a straight 1 to 1 mapping. We have looked through all their menu line and site configuration and all menu lines and meal types are set to OVS on. So we will be updating all breakfast and lunch menus to turn OVS on for all serving groups that are available on the menu. We will also update on the plans/records that have been assigned to reflect this information.
turns.body_html:
All, 
We realized the the OVS flag in PE is on the menu line and meal type per site. In SC we associate the OVS on the menu itself therefore there is not a straight 1 to 1 mapping. We have looked through all their menu line and site configuration and all menu lines and meal types are set to OVS on. So we will be updating all breakfast and lunch menus to turn OVS on for all serving groups that are available on the menu. We will also update on the plans/records that have been assigned to reflect this information.

Hello Ashley,     Thank you for the call. You can reply here with the screenshot.  Subsequent to our call, I used the scale recipe to 1 serving only to compare no 8 scoop to cup and was seeing it rate 1/2 cup as 2 2/5 ounce but No 8 as 1 1/5 ounce. Looking at online conversions it looks like 1 no. 8 scoop is roughly 4ounce dry/liquid (however, I do know dry can of course vary, as sugar is denser than flour).  It does look like the system for the conversion of No. 8 scoop in the scale recipe is using the standard 4ounce, where 4ounce =~113 grams.  This recipe is tagged as 68grams and 2ounces (what seems half of 1/2 cup).  But, I also can see when I search online conversions that if using carrots grated, dry it does advise it is 2oz. for 1/2 cup or 4 ounces for 1 cup.              This provides a conflict due to density and thus why the 1/2 cup measure at 2ounces is rating for only half of 1 no.8 scoop with the standard being 4oz.   I am escalating to inquire further about scaling conversion in this example. turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Ashley,     We are escalating to product and development for review and correction of the scale recipe under production.  ​   To clarify the situation, in reviewing further with our product team we found it is not related to an issue of density differences (please disregard my prior reference on that). The system uses the weight provided on the recipe when it converts to the other measures by using the standard ratio so if 1/2 cup = 1 no 8 scoop then if 1/2 cup = 2oz the no 8 scoop also weights 2oz.  We can't see the conversion on the front end, but it is there. Instead, the issue is the Scale recipe feature on the Production side does not appear to be using one of the fields and instead is defaulting always to the serving measure on the recipe, so in this case "1/2", which is why it keeps saying "1/2 no 8 scoop". reference screenshot:          However, it is using the fields and information correctly from underneath the Recipe itself for scaling and Print Recipes. While this is being corrected, we would recommend you use the feature through here for now:               Separately though, a few things you may want to consider that were seen in our discussion:   You may want to review some of how recipes are being created.  You may notice on recipe 0638 it provides just 210cups of all ingredient. That is because it is using a single sub-recipe as its ingredient. Where as if you use the Recipe LR-0589 in scaling you can see how many carrots vs celery as this is using actual ingredients with instructions on each.  It may serve you better to have the ingredients directly on the recipe.    Also, we suggest reviewing recipes where a CN item is used.  Generally we recommend referencing CN items only as a guide and to always make a copy of it instead which you can edit as needed. Many of those recipes are only for nutritionals and do not have all sizes, documents, or other you may need. Also you cannot edit them if ever needed.  They are meant more as a reference of nutritionals than to be used as the ingredient for the recipes. This is particularly relevant when using Production or as you move into using Inventory and will want to withdraw stock. So we do recommend reviewing and changing over ingredients in any recipes using a CN item. training courses in the Primeroedge Academy
turns.body_html:
Hello Ashley,

We are escalating to product and development for review and correction of the scale recipe under production.
​
To clarify the situation, in reviewing further with our product team we found it is not related to an issue of density differences (please disregard my prior reference on that). The system uses the weight provided on the recipe when it converts to the other measures by using the standard ratio so if 1/2 cup = 1 no 8 scoop then if 1/2 cup = 2oz the no 8 scoop also weights 2oz.  We can't see the conversion on the front end, but it is there. Instead, the issue is the Scale recipe feature on the Production side does not appear to be using one of the fields and instead is defaulting always to the serving measure on the recipe, so in this case "1/2", which is why it keeps saying "1/2 no 8 scoop". reference screenshot:


However, it is using the fields and information correctly from underneath the Recipe itself for scaling and Print Recipes. While this is being corrected, we would recommend you use the feature through here for now:

Separately though, a few things you may want to consider that were seen in our discussion:
  1. You may want to review some of how recipes are being created.  You may notice on recipe 0638 it provides just 210cups of all ingredient. That is because it is using a single sub-recipe as its ingredient. Where as if you use the Recipe LR-0589 in scaling you can see how many carrots vs celery as this is using actual ingredients with instructions on each.  It may serve you better to have the ingredients directly on the recipe.  
  2. Also, we suggest reviewing recipes where a CN item is used.  Generally we recommend referencing CN items only as a guide and to always make a copy of it instead which you can edit as needed. Many of those recipes are only for nutritionals and do not have all sizes, documents, or other you may need. Also you cannot edit them if ever needed.  They are meant more as a reference of nutritionals than to be used as the ingredient for the recipes. This is particularly relevant when using Production or as you move into using Inventory and will want to withdraw stock. So we do recommend reviewing and changing over ingredients in any recipes using a CN item.

subject: Old Colony Regional Vocational Technical - Flat Bread Inventory Count

turns.author: Crystal Andrade <candrade@oldcolony.us>
turns.body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.

Good Morning,

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Crystal,     Is the concern you have with seeing your total inventory value jump from $19,301.79 (in your Feb 3rd report ) to $21,076.80 (in your current report )?      I want to explain exactly why the numbers changed so you can trust this new report.     The report you saved on Feb 3rd was generated while the inventory period was still Pending Reconciliation.     Before Reconciliation: The system provides an estimate of your inventory value based on the previous month's costs. It effectively takes a "snapshot" without fully processing all the new pricing data from the current month.    After Reconciliation: When you hit "Reconcile," the system triggers a hard recalculation. It looks at every invoice entered in January, updates the "Unit Cost" for each item based on what you actually paid this month (using Weighted Average or FIFO costing), and applies that accurate price to your counts.    The increase of roughly $1,775  suggests that the invoices processed in January had higher unit costs than the system's previous estimates.   ​The $21,076.80 figure is the accurate financial number that reflects the true value of the goods sitting on your shelves right now.  ​   ​           Check out PrimeroEdge Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here  SchoolCafe Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here    Let us know if you have any questions regarding this ticket by
replying all to this ticket         Keith Noury| CYBERSOFT   Region Lead, Customer and Expert Care   Support: 866.442.6030 | Email: customercare@primeroedge.com   PrimeroEdge | SchoolCafé       Let’s Chat …Send an
email to customercare@primeroedge.com to enable your PrimeroEdge support LIVE CHAT feature      Check out our refresher training courses in the PrimeroEdge
Academy
turns.body_html:
Hello Crystal,

Is the concern you have with seeing your total inventory value jump from $19,301.79 (in your Feb 3rd report ) to $21,076.80 (in your current report )? 

I want to explain exactly why the numbers changed so you can trust this new report.

The report you saved on Feb 3rd was generated while the inventory period was still Pending Reconciliation.

  • Before Reconciliation: The system provides an estimate of your inventory value based on the previous month's costs. It effectively takes a "snapshot" without fully processing all the new pricing data from the current month.
  • After Reconciliation: When you hit "Reconcile," the system triggers a hard recalculation. It looks at every invoice entered in January, updates the "Unit Cost" for each item based on what you actually paid this month (using Weighted Average or FIFO costing), and applies that accurate price to your counts.
The increase of roughly $1,775  suggests that the invoices processed in January had higher unit costs than the system's previous estimates.

​The $21,076.80 figure is the accurate financial number that reflects the true value of the goods sitting on your shelves right now.
​
​

turns.author: agent:22001856480
turns.body_text: I am requesting assistance to clarify the system behavior for this customer. I do not have any other explinations that I can provide to her.      The customer noticed discrepancies in her January inventory. I advised her to reconcile the month, which updated the reports. However, she is now reporting that "it looks like it removed some of what we counted" after she hit reconcile. She believes the system is overwriting her physical entry counts with calculated data.     I explained how reconciliation updates the Inventory Valuation (Unit Costs/Financials) based on invoice processing (Snapshot vs. Actual). However, the customer is specifically asking why her Physical Counts (Quantities) appeared to change or be ignored. She stated: "We entered information into the inventory counter... I am not sure why it did not use that information."     Please review January Inventory (specifically the "Flat Bread" LI-0149 item).    Does the reconciliation process in SchoolCafe ever overwrite a manually entered physical count with a theoretical count (Perpetual Inventory)?    Please provide a simplified explanation we can give the customer that differentiates between how the system handles Quantity (User Input) vs. Value (System Calculation) during reconciliation.    The customer identified a discrepancy between the physical inventory counts her team entered in January (specifically regarding "Flat Bread") and what the system report was displaying. She noted that the count "did not look right" and provided screenshots showing the specific entered counts versus the report output.     After I instructed her to reconcile the inventory (which she did), the reporting issue appeared to resolve.      However, the customer became confused and concerned about why the data was wrong in the first place.     She stated, "I am not sure why it did not use that information [the entered counts]. Once I reconciled the month it looks like it removed some of what we counted".     Her fear is that the system is overwriting her manual physical counts during the reconciliation process. She explicitly stated she is "mostly concerned with the information I am entering not being calculated correctly or having it change once reconciled".     I identified that the January Inventory was "Pending Reconciliation" and instructed the customer to reconcile it to update the counts.     When the customer asked why the numbers changed and why the initial data didn't match, I provided a detailed technical explanation regarding Inventory Valuation rather than Inventory Counts (Quantities).     I explained that before reconciliation, the system takes a "snapshot" estimate based on previous cost and that reconciliation triggers a "hard recalculation" that updates the "Unit Cost" based on actual January invoices using Weighted Average or FIFO costing.     I assumed she was concerned about the financial value increase (jumping from $19,301.79 to $21,076.80)     However, after explanation the valuation and unit costs, it failed to address the customer's specific fear: that her physical quantity counts (e.g., number of cases/bags) were being altered or ignored.     The customer is asking about "Counts" and I was explaining "Value" Price.     First screenshot was the counts THEY entered in January inventory and the second one is the report                   After The inventory was reconciled  The customer even states "I just did and it is reporting correctly now.  Did you have to do something?  How long should I wait to generate a report if not?"                Then she came back with "Why would it have had the wrong information to begin with? It did not match the information entered."
turns.body_html:
I am requesting assistance to clarify the system behavior for this customer. I do not have any other explinations that I can provide to her. 

The customer noticed discrepancies in her January inventory. I advised her to reconcile the month, which updated the reports. However, she is now reporting that "it looks like it removed some of what we counted" after she hit reconcile. She believes the system is overwriting her physical entry counts with calculated data.

I explained how reconciliation updates the Inventory Valuation (Unit Costs/Financials) based on invoice processing (Snapshot vs. Actual). However, the customer is specifically asking why her Physical Counts (Quantities) appeared to change or be ignored. She stated: "We entered information into the inventory counter... I am not sure why it did not use that information."

Please review January Inventory (specifically the "Flat Bread" LI-0149 item).
  1. Does the reconciliation process in SchoolCafe ever overwrite a manually entered physical count with a theoretical count (Perpetual Inventory)?
  2. Please provide a simplified explanation we can give the customer that differentiates between how the system handles Quantity (User Input) vs. Value (System Calculation) during reconciliation.
The customer identified a discrepancy between the physical inventory counts her team entered in January (specifically regarding "Flat Bread") and what the system report was displaying. She noted that the count "did not look right" and provided screenshots showing the specific entered counts versus the report output.

After I instructed her to reconcile the inventory (which she did), the reporting issue appeared to resolve. 

However, the customer became confused and concerned about why the data was wrong in the first place.

She stated, "I am not sure why it did not use that information [the entered counts]. Once I reconciled the month it looks like it removed some of what we counted".

Her fear is that the system is overwriting her manual physical counts during the reconciliation process. She explicitly stated she is "mostly concerned with the information I am entering not being calculated correctly or having it change once reconciled".

I identified that the January Inventory was "Pending Reconciliation" and instructed the customer to reconcile it to update the counts.

When the customer asked why the numbers changed and why the initial data didn't match, I provided a detailed technical explanation regarding Inventory Valuation rather than Inventory Counts (Quantities).

I explained that before reconciliation, the system takes a "snapshot" estimate based on previous cost and that reconciliation triggers a "hard recalculation" that updates the "Unit Cost" based on actual January invoices using Weighted Average or FIFO costing.

I assumed she was concerned about the financial value increase (jumping from $19,301.79 to $21,076.80)

However, after explanation the valuation and unit costs, it failed to address the customer's specific fear: that her physical quantity counts (e.g., number of cases/bags) were being altered or ignored.

The customer is asking about "Counts" and I was explaining "Value" Price.

First screenshot was the counts THEY entered in January inventory and the second one is the report 




After The inventory was reconciled
The customer even states "I just did and it is reporting correctly now.  Did you have to do something?  How long should I wait to generate a report if not?"




Then she came back with "Why would it have had the wrong information to begin with? It did not match the information entered."

Reconciliation overwrites the perpetual value with the values provided on the Physical Inventory. That's all it does. When they Reconcile, the Physical Value will become the new Perpetual Value  This is true for every item that is counted on a Physical Inventory, even if the new value is zero. turns.body_html:
Guys, respectfully I really don't think these AI based summaries are helping. There's SO much text in this ticket and much of it is simply misinformation. I'm not sure where all this stuff about invoices and FIFO and whatnot is coming from. That's irrelevant to this process.

Reconciliation overwrites the perpetual value with the values provided on the Physical Inventory. That's all it does. It simply replaces the perpetual value with the physical value. 


  • Before they did their physical count, the system had 3 Case, 8 Bags and 6 Flatbreads.
  • When they did their physical count, they entered 0 Case, 3 Bag and 6 Flatbreads.
  • When they Reconcile, the Physical Value will become the new Perpetual Value
  • This is true for every item that is counted on a Physical Inventory, even if the new value is zero.
I'm not sure what else there is to say about this. I think there is some other confusion going on here. If you want to set up a call with the customer I'd be glad to make myself available. Otherwise I think we may keep running in circles on this, because I'm not super clear on what issue is being reported.

turns.body_html:
Hi Crystal,

​When you reconcile a physical inventory, you are replacing your perpetual inventory values with the physical inventory values. 


  • Before you did the physical count, the system had 3 Case, 8 Bags and 6 Flatbreads
  • When you did the physical count, you entered 0 Case, 3 Bag and 6 Flatbreads
  • When you Reconciled, the Physical Value became the new Perpetual Value. 
  • This is true for every item that is counted on a Physical Inventory, even if the new value is zero
I've looked into your January physical inventory and everything looks correct to me. I'm not entirely clear on what issue is being reported. On Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 1:46 PM customercare@primeroedge.com <customercare@primeroedge.com> wrote:
Hello Crystal,

Is the concern you have with seeing your total inventory value jump from $19,301.79 (in your Feb 3rd report ) to $21,076.80 (in your current report )? 

I want to explain exactly why the numbers changed so you can trust this new report.

The report you saved on Feb 3rd was generated while the inventory period was still Pending Reconciliation.

  • Before Reconciliation: The system provides an estimate of your inventory value based on the previous month's costs. It effectively takes a "snapshot" without fully processing all the new pricing data from the current month.
  • After Reconciliation: When you hit "Reconcile," the system triggers a hard recalculation. It looks at every invoice entered in January, updates the "Unit Cost" for each item based on what you actually paid this month (using Weighted Average or FIFO costing), and applies that accurate price to your counts.
The increase of roughly $1,775  suggests that the invoices processed in January had higher unit costs than the system's previous estimates.

​The $21,076.80 figure is the accurate financial number that reflects the true value of the goods sitting on your shelves right now.
​
​

turns.author: agent:22052027946
turns.body_text: District is reporting that the same menu cycle shows different nutrient values when different serving groups are selected, even though the serving group should not affect the nutrients.      Menu Cycle: QIS March Lunch Week 2 2026   When the K-8 serving group is selected, the weekly analysis shows values of 600.519 kcals for calories, 6.192 g for saturated fat, 863.238 mg for sodium, and 18.745 g for added sugars.   When the K-5 serving group is selected, the weekly analysis shows values of 675.636 kcals for calories, 6.778 g for saturated fat, 994.567 mg for sodium, and 20.295 g for added sugars.   Additionally, the daily analysis values do not match when the serving group is changed.                  Checked this issue for Menus as well. The same thing is replicated for Menus too.     Menu: QIS March Lunch Week 2 2026 Week 1 Day 1 -   When selected K8 as serving group shows values of calories 606.336, Sat Fat 8.422 g, Sodium 977.578 mg, Ad sugars 15.880 g.   When K5 is selected as serving group, shows values of calories 606.606, Sat Fat 8.407 g, Sodium 972.918 mg, Ad sugars 15.660 g.               Yolissa created a menu copy (copy of QIS March Lunch Week 2 2026 Week 1 Day 1) to compare the nutrient reports when serving groups are changed. Every single value remained the same regardless of whether the menu was for K-8 or K-5. Attached both nutrient analysis reports for review
turns.body_html:
District is reporting that the same menu cycle shows different nutrient values when different serving groups are selected, even though the serving group should not affect the nutrients. 

Menu Cycle: QIS March Lunch Week 2 2026 
When the K-8 serving group is selected, the weekly analysis shows values of 600.519 kcals for calories, 6.192 g for saturated fat, 863.238 mg for sodium, and 18.745 g for added sugars. 
When the K-5 serving group is selected, the weekly analysis shows values of 675.636 kcals for calories, 6.778 g for saturated fat, 994.567 mg for sodium, and 20.295 g for added sugars. 
Additionally, the daily analysis values do not match when the serving group is changed.




Checked this issue for Menus as well. The same thing is replicated for Menus too.

Menu: QIS March Lunch Week 2 2026 Week 1 Day 1 - 
When selected K8 as serving group shows values of calories 606.336, Sat Fat 8.422 g, Sodium 977.578 mg, Ad sugars 15.880 g. 
When K5 is selected as serving group, shows values of calories 606.606, Sat Fat 8.407 g, Sodium 972.918 mg, Ad sugars 15.660 g.



Yolissa created a menu copy (copy of QIS March Lunch Week 2 2026 Week 1 Day 1) to compare the nutrient reports when serving groups are changed. Every single value remained the same regardless of whether the menu was for K-8 or K-5. Attached both nutrient analysis reports for review

turns.author: agent:22005101122
turns.body_text: The district is having an issue with their carried over item MI-0210 showing on production record 1/14/26 carry over as coming from the carried over amounts on 1/6/26, however on      1/6/26 they produced 25 of MI-0210 and carried over 22 to Wednesday 1/7/26 meaning they served 3 on 1/6/26 -  then  1/7/26 they carried over 15 to Thursday 1/8/26 meaning they served  7 on 1/7/26 - then  1/8/26 they carried over 11 to Friday 1/9/26 meaning they served 4 on 1/8/26 - then   1/9/26 they carried over 9 to Monday 1/12/26 meaning on 1/9/26 they served 2 then on Monday  1/12/26 they served all 9 that were carried over from Friday 1/9/26 so Tuesday   1/13/26 they planned for 10, produced 16, served 4 and carried over 12 to Wednesday 1/14/26   On Wednesday 1/14/26 shows the 12 carried over though is also showing in the below screen shot Originally from 1/6/26 and a max carryover of 4   The district wants to know why it is showing Originally from 1/6/26 when all 22 of those carried over items from 1/6/26 were already served by 1/13/26 and newly produced items as of 1/14/26?        Please see above for detailed screen shots showing these accounts
turns.body_html:
The district is having an issue with their carried over item MI-0210 showing on production record 1/14/26 carry over as coming from the carried over amounts on 1/6/26, however on 

1/6/26 they produced 25 of MI-0210 and carried over 22 to Wednesday 1/7/26 meaning they served 3 on 1/6/26 -  then
1/7/26 they carried over 15 to Thursday 1/8/26 meaning they served  7 on 1/7/26 - then
1/8/26 they carried over 11 to Friday 1/9/26 meaning they served 4 on 1/8/26 - then 
1/9/26 they carried over 9 to Monday 1/12/26 meaning on 1/9/26 they served 2 then on Monday
1/12/26 they served all 9 that were carried over from Friday 1/9/26 so Tuesday 
1/13/26 they planned for 10, produced 16, served 4 and carried over 12 to Wednesday 1/14/26 
On Wednesday 1/14/26 shows the 12 carried over though is also showing in the below screen shot Originally from 1/6/26 and a max carryover of 4 
The district wants to know why it is showing Originally from 1/6/26 when all 22 of those carried over items from 1/6/26 were already served by 1/13/26 and newly produced items as of 1/14/26?


Please see above for detailed screen shots showing these accounts

turns.author: agent:22052027946
turns.body_text: 703 - Future School of Fort Smith | Lunch - Quick Launch (1) - 01/14     On 01/14, there are 12 counts of Lettuce Tomato Setup (MI-0210) CARRIED OVER from 01/13. All of these were produced on 01/13.     The district is attempting to carry forward 5 of these items from 01/14 to 01/15. However, the system is displaying the carried from date as 01/14/2026 (Originally from 01/06/2026), which is incorrect. These items are originally from 01/13/2026. Due to this discrepancy, the items cannot be carried forward.     All 25 units of the menu item "Lettuce Tomato Setup MI-0210" produced on 01/06 were depleted by 01/12. An additional 10 units were produced on 01/13.      The district is seeking an explanation for this issue.     Screenshot from Production record dated 01/12: The system mentions the item MI-0210 is carried over from 01/09.          Screenshot from Production record dated 01/13: The system does not mention any carryovers. These items are newly produced on 01/13.           Below are the screenshots of the Production record from 01/14 : When the district attempts to carry over these menu items to 01/15, they receive an error message stating that the items are from 01/06, which is not the case. The items were produced on 01/13
turns.body_html:
703 - Future School of Fort Smith | Lunch - Quick Launch (1) - 01/14

On 01/14, there are 12 counts of Lettuce Tomato Setup (MI-0210) CARRIED OVER from 01/13. All of these were produced on 01/13.

The district is attempting to carry forward 5 of these items from 01/14 to 01/15. However, the system is displaying the carried from date as 01/14/2026 (Originally from 01/06/2026), which is incorrect. These items are originally from 01/13/2026. Due to this discrepancy, the items cannot be carried forward.

All 25 units of the menu item "Lettuce Tomato Setup MI-0210" produced on 01/06 were depleted by 01/12. An additional 10 units were produced on 01/13. 

The district is seeking an explanation for this issue.

Screenshot from Production record dated 01/12: The system mentions the item MI-0210 is carried over from 01/09.


Screenshot from Production record dated 01/13: The system does not mention any carryovers. These items are newly produced on 01/13. 


Below are the screenshots of the Production record from 01/14 : When the district attempts to carry over these menu items to 01/15, they receive an error message stating that the items are from 01/06, which is not the case. The items were produced on 01/13

subject: SC Implementation - All Districts - Set All Prices Entry Issue

turns.author: Kelsey Brown <kelsey.brown@cybersoft.net>
turns.body_text: When trying to set $0.50 as the price in the Set Price for All entry box, it will not allow you to enter the decimal first. Once I enter a number, I can then add the decimal in front of the initial number. I also found that when I clicked the Backspace button, it only partially deleted the number before, and no longer allowed the remaining numbers to be deleted. I had to move the cursor using the arrow buttons a few times to allow the number to be deleted via the backspace key. I tested this in Caring and the Arkansas Academy of the Arts. Recording attached.

turns.author: agent:22024854769
turns.body_text: I was able to replicate both issues being reported.        The Apply All price field on the Menu Item price lists will not allow you to enter .50, where all the fields below do allow it.    See in my recording where I first entered .50 in a field below, but when I tried in the Apply All field, it will not accept the period first.    Second issue, is it will allow you to enter a number, then enter a decimal, but then you can't delete the value after that. That is how the recording ends.
turns.body_html:
I was able to replicate both issues being reported.  

  • The Apply All price field on the Menu Item price lists will not allow you to enter .50, where all the fields below do allow it.  
    • See in my recording where I first entered .50 in a field below, but when I tried in the Apply All field, it will not accept the period first.
  • Second issue, is it will allow you to enter a number, then enter a decimal, but then you can't delete the value after that. That is how the recording ends. 

  •

turns.author: agent:22001856315
turns.body_text: Hillcrest Elementary and Coyote Ridge have reported that Production Record counts are not saving as expected. They report that when they enter all item counts, then save, there is a long load time and all counts are deleted. They can add counts one by one and save between items, but it is time-consuming. There was a previous ticket, #304177. Screen recording attached

turns.body_text: - District is requesting we open or change the status on all Reconciled October Inventories to Unreconciled.  - I don't see an option to do this from my side.  - They state they have a lot of discrepancies entered by staff
turns.body_html:
- District is requesting we open or change the status on all Reconciled October Inventories to Unreconciled.
- I don't see an option to do this from my side.
- They state they have a lot of discrepancies entered by staff

turns.author: agent:22004527326
turns.body_text: (Very internal response here)     This is the 3rd request from Waller in recent weeks to unreconcile an inventory for them. It's certainly my fault for not better anticipating this and providing users a way to rollback the reconciliations themselves. However, I really have to stress that clearly they are not using Physical Inventory in the way it's intended to be used. We have many tools for reviewing inventories, catching discrepancies, re-opening and correcting inventories, etc. But all of this must be done before reconciliation. Reconciliation is a big, super important function. It is a permanent reset on their perpetual. It should not be used lightly. We likely need to reach out and talk with the customer to determine if their process if conflicting with how we anticipate Reconciliation to be utilized.      The expectation is that discrepancies like this are caught before reconciling. We have a Discrepancy "view" on this page, as well as a dedicated Discrepancy Report.           In the meantime:   I'll request Development to once again manually revert these reconciliations. We cannot make a habit of this though.   We need to re-communicate how we expect Physical Inventory & Reconciliation to be used  We will look into adding an option directly in the website for users to do this themselves, if possible.   We have a pretty good relationship with Waller so I think they'll be receptive to a conversation about this. Let me know if you'd like to lead that or if you want me to reach out directly. Thanks!
turns.body_html:
(Very internal response here)

This is the 3rd request from Waller in recent weeks to unreconcile an inventory for them. It's certainly my fault for not better anticipating this and providing users a way to rollback the reconciliations themselves. However, I really have to stress that clearly they are not using Physical Inventory in the way it's intended to be used. We have many tools for reviewing inventories, catching discrepancies, re-opening and correcting inventories, etc. But all of this must be done before reconciliation. Reconciliation is a big, super important function. It is a permanent reset on their perpetual. It should not be used lightly. We likely need to reach out and talk with the customer to determine if their process if conflicting with how we anticipate Reconciliation to be utilized. 

The expectation is that discrepancies like this are caught before reconciling. We have a Discrepancy "view" on this page, as well as a dedicated Discrepancy Report. 


In the meantime:
  • I'll request Development to once again manually revert these reconciliations. We cannot make a habit of this though. 
  • We need to re-communicate how we expect Physical Inventory & Reconciliation to be used
  • We will look into adding an option directly in the website for users to do this themselves, if possible.
We have a pretty good relationship with Waller so I think they'll be receptive to a conversation about this. Let me know if you'd like to lead that or if you want me to reach out directly. Thanks!

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Kristie,  I went ahead and looked into this with Justin so we can get everything sorted out for October. We've already asked Development to roll back the reconciliations so your team can make the adjustments they need.  Just a quick reminder, reconciliation is meant to be the very last step, so once it’s done, the system treats it as final. We’re happy to help this time, but we want to make sure it doesn’t keep creating extra headaches for you all each month. It might help us to do a quick refresher with the managers on reviewing counts and catching discrepancies before reconciling, just so nobody gets stuck afterward.  The good news is that Justin is going to look into adding a way for districts to unreconcile on their own in the future. That should make things a lot easier and give you more control over the process without needing to wait on us. I don't have an ETA on that yet, but hopefully it will help prevent having to request it in the future.   If you want, I can set up a quick call and walk through the workflow with whoever needs it on how to review before reconciling. Whatever helps make this smoother for you going forward. :)  Just let me know!     Thank you,        Yolissa
Cumba | CYBERSOFT  Coordinator,

Just a quick reminder, reconciliation is meant to be the very last step, so once it’s done, the system treats it as final. We’re happy to help this time, but we want to make sure it doesn’t keep creating extra headaches for you all each month. It might help us to do a quick refresher with the managers on reviewing counts and catching discrepancies before reconciling, just so nobody gets stuck afterward.

The good news is that Justin is going to look into adding a way for districts to unreconcile on their own in the future. That should make things a lot easier and give you more control over the process without needing to wait on us. subject: SC Implementation - Southwest ISD- Problem importing updated Item Import

turns.author: Selena Nimchan <selena.nimchan@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text: Good Afternoon,     I am trying to import a file for Southwest ISD.  They completed the Menu Planning Module and are starting with Inventory. Their ingredients were exported and we were told that they could use the attached spreadsheet to import items with updated pack sizes.  When attempting to import, the file fails because it is looking for the "Is a food item" column.  When that column is added, the import fails because it's looking for the nutritionals.
turns.body_html:
Good Afternoon,

I am trying to import a file for Southwest ISD.  They completed the Menu Planning Module and are starting with Inventory. turns.body_html:
Jessica, 

Just FYI. The items in the Item Onboarding tool will never appear in the local database, because the items in this tool have not been created until they are Published at the end of the tool. They are in a temp table until that point.

But more importantly, those two items should be appearing on the Weights tab of the Pack-Serving Link step. I'm not sure why they aren't, but this tool is in beta so I'm not super surprised we're having some issues. We'll take a look into it as soon as possible. Thank you.

turns.author: agent:22004527326
turns.body_text: BTW, if the "12 each" in the pack size is referring to the Beef Patty, then the config we see in the screenshot will not create the ideal data.          The above scenario will produce the following hierarchy:   Case  x12 Each (BU)  1 Each (Serving)       We actually don't allow multiple units to have the same description so this will produce an error at some point anyway.     So if those "each" are the same, you'd either want to:   Click the 'Use Serving as Base Unit' toggle, making the patty part of the pack sizes itself. They probably don't want to count individual patties though.  OR, Change the Pack Size to 1 unit instead of 2, so it's just "Case" then you could use the 'Serving per Base Unit' field to numerically link them.    Furthermore, the data on the Weights tab doesn't make sense. If the Base Unit is 2 Ounces, but the Serving Size is 4 Ounces, that means there are two base units for each serving? That's very unusual. Either they should be the same weight (meaning it's a dual unit) or the pack size should be at least 2x the serving size.       I know this is test data and not super serious! But since we don't have all our validations and all our logic programmed into the tool, we will be trying to help steer things. I know it's pretty confusing still. Let me know if I can do anything to make better sense of all this. Thank you!
turns.body_html:
BTW, if the "12 each" in the pack size is referring to the Beef Patty, then the config we see in the screenshot will not create the ideal data.


The above scenario will produce the following hierarchy:
  • Case
    • x12 Each (BU)
      • 1 Each (Serving)
We actually don't allow multiple units to have the same description so this will produce an error at some point anyway.

So if those "each" are the same, you'd either want to:
  • Click the 'Use Serving as Base Unit' toggle, making the patty part of the pack sizes itself. They probably don't want to count individual patties though.
  • OR, Change the Pack Size to 1 unit instead of 2, so it's just "Case" then you could use the 'Serving per Base Unit' field to numerically link them. 
Furthermore, the data on the Weights tab doesn't make sense. If the Base Unit is 2 Ounces, but the Serving Size is 4 Ounces, that means there are two base units for each serving? That's very unusual. Either they should be the same weight (meaning it's a dual unit) or the pack size should be at least 2x the serving size.

I know this is test data and not super serious! But since we don't have all our validations and all our logic programmed into the tool, we will be trying to help steer things. I know it's pretty confusing still. Let me know if I can do anything to make better sense of all this. Thank you!

turns.author: agent:22052659220
turns.body_text: Changing the serving size to the base unit pack size fixed this. Thank you for explaining. Makes sense!
turns.body_html: Changing the serving size to the base unit pack size fixed this. Thank you for explaining. Makes sense!

turns.body_text: The district users are again reporting that when trying to add an item in production, all previously entered counts were deleted. subject: SC Implementation - Mexico School District 59 - Menu Item Category not populating under Menu Info

turns.author: Hayden Foreman <hforeman@mexico.k12.mo.us>
turns.body_text: Good morning,     Mexico School District is trying to add new categories (namely A La Carte) to their Menu Item Category.  The category was added twice, as a menu and POS item, and as a menu item only.  The new Menu Item Category is not populating in Item Details in their Menu Information.  The only categories available from the dropdown are Condiments, Entree, Fruit, Grain, Milk and Vegetable.
turns.body_html:
Good morning,

Mexico School District is trying to add new categories (namely A La Carte) to their Menu Item Category.  The category was added twice, as a menu and POS item, and as a menu item only.  The new Menu Item Category is not populating in Item Details in their Menu Information.  The only categories available from the dropdown are Condiments, Entree, Fruit, Grain, Milk and Vegetable.

turns.author: agent:22028160881
turns.body_text: Hi Jaime Lawrence & Jessica Bockholt   We have resolved the issue regarding menu item categories not being immediately available when added or modified. The problem was due to caching not resetting for 24 hours. This has now been resolved and verified in production. The cache will immediately reset to include the modifications. Please verify once and update the customer accordingly.      Thank you!
turns.body_html:
Hi Jaime Lawrence & Jessica Bockholt 

We have resolved the issue regarding menu item categories not being immediately available when added or modified. The problem was due to caching not resetting for 24 hours. This has now been resolved and verified in production. The cache will immediately reset to include the modifications. Please verify once and update the customer accordingly. 

Thank you!

subject: Arkansas Arts Academy- Assistance Needed with School Café Menu Planning

turns.author: Emily Karnes <ekarnes@artsk12.org>
turns.body_text: Hello,         Please see below, reported by Emily Karnes at the Arkansas Arts Academy. See previous tickets on this same issue.         I’m still experiencing issues with building my menus. When I go into Cycle Menu, I add all of my menu items to each day and then select “Save and Close.” However, when I return to that week, everything I entered is erased.    Could you please help me resolve this issue?    Thank you,    Jill                                From: Emily Karnes <ekarnes@artsk12.org>   Sent: Thursday, September 18, 2025 1:21 PM  To: Jill Boe <Jill.Boe@cybersoft.net>  Subject: [EXT]Re: [EXT]Re: [EXT]Assistance Needed with School Café Menu Planning            CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.       Good Afternoon,             I’m still experiencing issues with building my menus. When I go into Cycle Menu, I add all of my menu items to each day and then select “Save and Close.” However, when I return to that week, everything I entered is erased.      Could you please help me resolve this issue?    Thank you,             On Thu, Sep 11, 2025 at 3:50 PM Jill Boe <Jill.Boe@cybersoft.net> wrote:         You as well Emily!!                      From: Emily Karnes <ekarnes@artsk12.org>   Sent: Thursday, September 11, 2025 1:49 PM  To: Jill Boe <Jill.Boe@cybersoft.net>  Subject: [EXT]Re: [EXT]Assistance Needed with School Café Menu Planning            CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.       Thank you Jill. I will.             Have a great evening!             On Thu, Sep 11, 2025 at 3:22 PM Jill Boe <Jill.Boe@cybersoft.net> wrote:         Hi Emily,         I am so sorry you are having these issues. I have escalated this to management for review.          Please let me know if you do not get a response within 24 hours.         Thank you,    Jill                           From: Emily Karnes <ekarnes@artsk12.org>   Sent: Thursday, September 11, 2025 1:02 PM  To: Jill Boe <Jill.Boe@cybersoft.net>  Subject: [EXT]Assistance Needed with School Café Menu Planning            CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.        Good Afternoon,    I want to start by saying that Keori has been wonderful and always available when I need help. I truly appreciate her support.    That said, I am getting frustrated with the School Café website while trying to build my menus for this school year. It took quite a while to get through the Excel process and add all of our items, and now that I am past that phase, I am running into new challenges. It seems like there may be a glitch in the system—sometimes not all components are added correctly, and other times, after spending significant time entering menu items into a week, the screen suddenly goes white and I lose my work.    I understand that much of this may be system-related and out of your control. Keori currently has two tickets open on my behalf, but I am hoping for some additional guidance on how I can move forward and successfully create our menus for this school year.    Thank you for your time and support. Could you please help me resolve this issue?

Jill

 Sent: Thursday, September 18, 2025 1:21 PM
 To: Jill Boe <Jill.Boe@cybersoft.net>

I’m still experiencing issues with building my menus. When I go into Cycle Menu, I add all of my menu items to each day and then select “Save and Close.” However, when I return to that week, everything I entered is erased.

From: Emily Karnes <ekarnes@artsk12.org>

Good Afternoon,
I want to start by saying that Keori has been wonderful and always available when I need help. I truly appreciate her support.
That said, I am getting frustrated with the School Café website while trying to build my menus for this school year. It took quite a while to get through the Excel process and add all of our items, and now that I am past that phase, I am running into new challenges. It seems like there may be a glitch in the system—sometimes not all components are added correctly, and other times, after spending significant time entering menu items into a week, the screen suddenly goes white and I lose my work.
I understand that much of this may be system-related and out of your control. Keori currently has two tickets open on my behalf, but I am hoping for some additional guidance on how I can move forward and successfully create our menus for this school year.
Thank you for your time and support.
Best regards,
 
-- 
Emily Karnes

Rogers, AR 72756

turns.body_text: - Escalating for review   Recipe: LR0999  Recipe call for 18 cups of chopped or sliced Tomatoes:    When you scale the recipe, for example 100 servings. it generates 576 tablespoons of tomatoes. It's not scaling correctly.     Tomatoes: The measurements looks correct.
turns.body_html:
- Escalating for review 

Recipe: LR0999

Recipe call for 18 cups of chopped or sliced Tomatoes:



When you scale the recipe, for example 100 servings. it generates 576 tablespoons of tomatoes. It's not scaling correctly. 



Tomatoes: The measurements looks correct.

turns.author: agent:22052027946
turns.body_text: Recipe: LR0999  The quantity of tomatoes (Item LI-0350) required for Taco Nacho Bowl (Recipe LR0999) is configured as 18 cups, chopped or sliced.          But, when you scale the recipe, for example to 100 servings, the report is generating tomatoes required in tablespoons.   In the report, it says 576 tablespoons of tomatoes are required for 100 servings of the recipe. It should be showing the quantity needed in cups.          Checked the units for the Item LI-0350, i.e., Tomatoes. There are no descriptions of it in tablespoons. Even so, the recipe details report is showing the required quantity in spoons.
turns.body_html:
Recipe: LR0999
The quantity of tomatoes (Item LI-0350) required for Taco Nacho Bowl (Recipe LR0999) is configured as 18 cups, chopped or sliced.


But, when you scale the recipe, for example to 100 servings, the report is generating tomatoes required in tablespoons. 
In the report, it says 576 tablespoons of tomatoes are required for 100 servings of the recipe. It should be showing the quantity needed in cups.


Checked the units for the Item LI-0350, i.e., Tomatoes. There are no descriptions of it in tablespoons. Even so, the recipe details report is showing the required quantity in spoons.

turns.body_html:
Recipe: LR0999
The quantity of tomatoes (Item LI-0350) required for Taco Nacho Bowl (Recipe LR0999) is configured as 18 cups, chopped or sliced.


But, when you scale the recipe, for example to 100 servings, the report is generating tomatoes required in tablespoons. 
In the report, it says 576 tablespoons of tomatoes are required for 100 servings of the recipe. It should be showing the quantity needed in cups.


Checked the units for the Item LI-0350, i.e., Tomatoes. There are no descriptions of it in tablespoons. Even so, the recipe details report is showing the required quantity in spoons.

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Lindsey,     We have verified that the daily requirements for both grain and meat have been met. However, it is important to note that daily and weekly requirements are separate. While the daily requirements are satisfied, the weekly analysis sums the grains from Day 1 to Day 5, and in this case, it is not meeting the weekly requirement. This is why the weekly analysis is failing.  The weekly grain requirement is [8–9], but in this case, the range is [5–11], which is causing the failure.  Please let us know if you need any further assistance. Thank you!          ✅ Complete your Year Begin Tasks ✨ Explore PrimeroEdge’s fresh look: reimagined.primeroedge.com ? See the Navigation

We are finding inconsistencies in the Student import mapping that is causing much confusion.  

For guardian emails, when mapping there is an option for Email and GuardianEmail.  Email is actually the guardian email, and GuardianEmail is not a working option.  




Secondly, when mapping Homerooms, the HomeroomNumber option will accept teacher names and numbers.  The HomeroomTeacher doesn't work at all.

Thank you!

subject: 2.0 Southwest ISD - Variety Fruit Production Plan Counts

turns.author: Ashley Choice <achoice11244@swisd.net>
turns.body_text: For some fruit items in the variety fruit, when toggling on, there is no way to enter counts.
turns.body_html: For some fruit items in the variety fruit, when toggling on, there is no way to enter counts.

turns.author: agent:22041608313
turns.body_text: I think she made changes to the menu after it was initially assigned. Changes did not carry over to Production automatically. Per Muhammad, they should carry over, and I should not suggest reassigning as a solution and should escalate this as it is an issue. Reference ticket: https://primeroedge.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/290426      Variety Fruit- On the menu, she has them broken into two menu items: Assorted Fruit 1 and Assorted Fruit 2  Highlighted examples do not have the option to enter counts when toggled on. All items with this issue are in the Assorted Fruits 2. All items within Assorted Fruits 1, generate the count boxes when toggled on.          Assorted Fruit 1- All show in Production and can enter counts when toggled on          Assorted Fruit 2: Cannot enter counts on plan when toggled on. Some items are not shoing in production. Ex. MI-0237
turns.body_html:
I think she made changes to the menu after it was initially assigned. Changes did not carry over to Production automatically. Per Muhammad, they should carry over, and I should not suggest reassigning as a solution and should escalate this as it is an issue. Reference ticket: https://primeroedge.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/290426 

Variety Fruit- On the menu, she has them broken into two menu items: Assorted Fruit 1 and Assorted Fruit 2
Highlighted examples do not have the option to enter counts when toggled on. All items with this issue are in the Assorted Fruits 2. All items within Assorted Fruits 1, generate the count boxes when toggled on. 


Assorted Fruit 1- All show in Production and can enter counts when toggled on


Assorted Fruit 2: Cannot enter counts on plan when toggled on. Some items are not shoing in production. Ex. MI-0237
