subject: SC - Waller ISD - Elementary Lunch Menu Calendar Report w Nutrients Error

turns.author: Kristie Iverson <kiverson@wallerisd.net>
turns.body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
 
 
 
 
 When we run the Lunch Menu Calendar Report with Nutrients for Elementary Schools we do not see the carbohydrate information for multiple menu items (entrees, vegetables, fruits, milk, and condiments).  For example we can see that chocolate milk has 18g
 on the breakfast menu calendar report for Elementary Schools, however it does not show any data on the lunch menu calendar report.   
 
 
 
 Attached are the Breakfast and Lunch Menu Calendar Reports for Waller ISD Elementary Schools.  Would someone be able to look into this please?   
 
 
 
 (I checked all of the JH and HS Lunch menus and the nutrient information is displaying properly for all breakfast and lunch menus) 
 
 
 
--  
 
 
 
Kristie Iverson
 
 
Assistant Director of School Nutrition - Waller ISD 
1918 Key Street
 
 
Waller, TX 77484
 
 
Phone: (936) 931-2347
 
 Fax: (936) 931-4047

turns.body_text:
Comment from Jira - Hi @Kalyan Patnala, could you please take a moment to review this PCS bug? If you're busy, feel free to reassign it to any member of the QA team.

The changes are currently in development, QA, UAT, and migration. Please ensure thorough testing is conducted across all environments.

*Root Cause:* When a Menu on the Assign Menus Calendar includes multiple Serving Groups, the nutrient values do not display if the first Serving Group is not served. This issue has been resolved to ensure that any Serving Measure amount greater than 0 in the available Serving Groups on the Menu is correctly considered.

*Dev Testing Demo:*

*Before Fix:*[^Menu Cycle Nutrient Summary Report (1).pdf] 

*After Fix:*[^Menu Cycle Nutrient Summary Report (2).pdf] 

Additionally, please log a separate bug to provide a proper report name. as of now its giving the Menu Cycle Nutrient Report 

@R Cherukuri @Sasirekha Kovvuru @Muhammad Aly

turns.body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.

It looks like the nutrient/carb information is now showing on the Elementary Lunch Menu.  Thank you for escalating our issue.

I noticed the "Breakfast Pancakes & Sausage Links" entree on 8/13/25 stated it has 0g carb.  When reviewing the recipe (Recipe LR0417) / Menu Item (MI-0417) it is only pulling the nutritional information for the sausage links... it is not taking into account or calculating the added nutrition from the 2 pancakes. Below is a snapshot of the recipe stating it includes 2 pancakes (picture 1), the incorrect nutritional information for the recipe (picture 2), and the nutritional information for the just pancakes (picture 3). Would someone be able to fix this please?  Thank you.

Picture 1: Recipe[image.png]
Picture 2: incorrect nutritional information of the recipe (only calculates 2 sausage links)
[image.png]

Picture 3: Nutritional information for 1 pancake
[image.png]

subject: Healdsburg Unified School District / Menu Planner Deleting Assigned Menus

turns.author: Leigh Ann Bozung <lbozung@husd.com>
turns.body_text: Customer called because they are working on cycles for the next few months, and in the process of doing so they noticed a few days which they had already completed and saved had nearly no items on it.
turns.body_html: Customer called because they are working on cycles for the next few months, and in the process of doing so they noticed a few days which they had already completed and saved had nearly no items on it.

turns.author: agent:22039013083
turns.body_text: Issue: Customer called because they are working on cycles for the next few months, and in the process of doing so they noticed a few days which they had already completed and saved had nearly no items on it.      Notes/Screenshots:     - Recorded customer working on cycles for over 15 min after clearing cache on her machine. We could not get it to replicate after this point but customer is insistent she didn't just make this up and has lost confidence in the software. She doesn't wanna work on menus if they can randomly disappear and she is convinced this is on us.     - Customer completed and assigned these cycles weeks ago. She has cleared cache multiple times since first assigning these. Example day 10/21/25:          - One of the menus where everything was deleted. Per her this menu had items on it before today. Now they are all gone. She put them back while we were on call, because she didn't want to wait on us to get around to fixing it, but is still demanding for us to do so. I tried to discuss with her about lack of evidence.       Escalating for review.
turns.body_html:

Escalating for review.

turns.author: agent:22028160881
turns.body_text: Hi Jaime,  Here is what we are currently doing for the “VMI Assorted Fruit” calorie calculation:     When “VMI Assorted Fruit” is selected in the menu, it isn’t known in advance which specific fruit will be served. Since this menu item represents a group of possible fruits, an average calorie value is calculated.     A standard serving size of 113.4 grams, as defined in the VMI, is used to ensure all fruits are evaluated on the same portion size.  The following fruits are included in the VMI Assorted Fruit group:   FruitCalories per 113.4g Zee Zee Applesauce  50 kcal  Fresh Apple Slices  28 kcal  Cupped Peaches  70 kcal  Raisins  480 kcal     Note: The calorie value for raisins is originally provided for a 28.35g (1-ounce) portion, which equals 120 kcal. To align with the 113.4g serving size, this value is scaled up by a factor of 4: 120 × 4 = 480 kcal     To determine the average: Sum of all fruits calories = 50 + 28+ 70 + 480 = 628  Average= 628/4=157 kcal  we currently assign 157 kcal per 113.4g serving to the VMI Assorted Fruit item.  Please let me know if you have any questions!
turns.body_html:
Hi Jaime,

Here is what we are currently doing for the “VMI Assorted Fruit” calorie calculation: 

When “VMI Assorted Fruit” is selected in the menu, it isn’t known in advance which specific fruit will be served. Since this menu item represents a group of possible fruits, an average calorie value is calculated.

A standard serving size of 113.4 grams, as defined in the VMI, is used to ensure all fruits are evaluated on the same portion size.
The following fruits are included in the VMI Assorted Fruit group:

Fruit Calories per 113.4g
Zee Zee Applesauce 50 kcal
Fresh Apple Slices 28 kcal
Cupped Peaches 70 kcal
Raisins 480 kcal

Note: The calorie value for raisins is originally provided for a 28.35g (1-ounce) portion, which equals 120 kcal. To align with the 113.4g serving size, this value is scaled up by a factor of 4:
120 × 4 = 480 kcal

To determine the average:
Sum of all fruits calories = 50 + 28+ 70 + 480 = 628
Average= 628/4=157 kcal
we currently assign 157 kcal per 113.4g serving to the VMI Assorted Fruit item.

Please let me know if you have any questions!

Hello Crystal,     For these items you can resolve the same way we did on the meeting. I did not see the butterbuds in the system but I sarted with Paprika so you can replicate.      Step 1: Find the item, and click the pencil in the top right of the Units section             Step 2: Click update and select inventory options           Step 3: Choose the correct base unit for inventory. turns.body_html:
Hello Crystal,

For these items you can resolve the same way we did on the meeting. I did not see the butterbuds in the system but I sarted with Paprika so you can replicate. 

Step 1: Find the item, and click the pencil in the top right of the Units section



Step 2: Click update and select inventory options



Step 3: Choose the correct base unit for inventory. Make sure it states the correct item for the other pack sizes to remove and of course choose by decimal or whole number, then confirm.



If you have any questions or additional information to share, please feel free to reply to this email and update the case.
 
Thank you!
